title: The Unwitting Witness
description: Two wrong publications sit on the board — one about Fern, one about Scorpion. A careful mix is worth more than two apprentice reveals.

hints.text: Two publications need to go: Fern (ingredient 1) claims pNn, and Scorpion (ingredient 6) claims nPp. Try to find a way to remove at least one without a plain apprentice reveal — a single master debunk might be cheaper than you think.

Mix Fern + Flower (ingredients 1 + 4). True result: R−.
The publication claims Fern is pNn. Predicted mix(pNn + nPp) = neutral ≠ R−, so there is a conflict on Fern's publication.
Flower has no wrong publication, so the conflict is unambiguous — Fern's publication is removed.

Now only Scorpion's publication remains. One apprentice reveal of R+ or B− on Scorpion finishes the job.

Optimal 2-step plan:
① Master debunk: mix Fern + Flower (1 + 4) → true result R−. Contradicts Fern's claimed pNn. Flower has no publication, so blame is unambiguous → Fern's publication removed.
② Apprentice debunk: reveal R+ (or B−) on Scorpion (6). Scorpion's true alchemical Ppn has R+; the publication claims nPp which has R−. Contradiction → Scorpion's publication removed.

Using two apprentice reveals instead (R− on Fern, then R+ or B− on Scorpion) also takes 2 steps and is equally valid.
